misconceptionSummary: Students express the remainder as a digit rather than showing it as a fraction or decimal of the divisor.

lines.text: Give the answer to 19 ÷ 5 as a mixed number
Remainder '4' is not a mixed number — needs fraction

steps.narration: Three remainder four is correct arithmetic, but the question asked for a mixed number. A mixed number has a whole part and a fraction part. The remainder four must be expressed as four fifths — four parts out of the five that make a whole.

visuals.ruleText: Remainder as a fraction: write remainder over the divisor

steps.narration: To convert a remainder into a fraction, write it over the divisor. The divisor tells you how many equal parts make one whole. Here, five equal parts make one — so the remaining four parts are four fifths.
Three and four fifths. Check: three times five is fifteen, plus four is nineteen — correct. In decimal form, four divided by five is zero point eight, so the answer is also three point eight.

visuals.label: Remainder over divisor gives the fraction part of the mixed number

steps.narration: Three and four fifths, or three point eight. Write the remainder over the divisor to form the fraction — the divisor tells you how many parts make one whole.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: leaving the answer as 3 r 4 when a mixed number is needed. The remainder becomes the numerator and the divisor becomes the denominator: 19 ÷ 5 = 3⁴⁄₅.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Remainder and mixed number are not the same thing. To write the remainder as a fraction, put it over the divisor. 19 ÷ 5 = 3 remainder 4 = 3 and four-fifths.
